step.title: Generate Scaffold goal (goal-002)
step.rule: Step 1 must have completed successfully (goal-001 created) before this step executes.
Scaffold is always goal-002 with phase=scaffold, depends_on=[goal-001] (G-02).
All inputs are read from docs/architecture/* files and templates on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).
See the <conventions> block in the spine.

substep.title: Resolve scaffold template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as SCAFFOLD_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used (overlay or base).
substep.rule: The MONOREPO topology (contexts/{BC}/src/{Domain,Application,Bundle}, contexts/{BC}/app framework layer, contexts/shared/src shared kernel, deployable projects/{app}/src, shared packages/) and the shared-kernel layout come from the resolved php-symfony conventions (monorepo-layout.md / context-layers.md / shared-kernel.md, loaded via the spine <conventions> block) and the resolved template overlay — never the retired flat src/<BC> skeleton, never hardcoded in this XML.

substep.title: Read architecture inputs
substep.action: Read the following files and extract structured data:

read.extract: All level-3 headings under "## Bounded Context Inventory" — these are the BC names for DDD directory structure and Deptrac layers.
Composer package names from the file. If file does not exist, fall back to .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/libraries.md.
.env.test variable names and values for SC-10.

substep.action: Read the monorepo deptrac pattern from the resolved php-symfony conventions (monorepo-layout.md / context-layers.md, loaded via the spine <conventions> block): the per-context LAYER chain (Domain ← Application ← Bundle ← app) AND the cross-package boundary edges (a context depends only on the shared-kernel context contexts/shared and packages/*, never on another context's internals; a deployable projects/{app} depends on the contexts it composes plus the shared kernel; packages/* are leaves). The resolved template (.tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/bounded-contexts.md) carries any project-specific overlay.
Read quality tool configs from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/quality-gates.md for PHPStan level 9, ECS, and Deptrac enforcement commands.

substep.title: Resolve source layout (docs/architecture/layout.md ## Layers)
substep.action: This substep runs BEFORE any `rules match` (substep 2.6b and every later code-rule injection): it resolves the `{src}`/`{infra}` tokens the code-rules engine globs against. Read docs/architecture/layout.md and extract its `## Layers` section — the two authoritative lines `- Source roots: <comma-joined globs>` and `- Infrastructure layer: <dir>` (authoritative over PSR-4 detection).

branch.condition: docs/architecture/layout.md is absent OR has no `## Layers` section
branch.action: CREATE docs/architecture/layout.md with a default monorepo `## Layers` block (facet-a fail-safe — never abort). Create docs/architecture/ first if absent. Write exactly:
branch.value:
## Layers
- Source roots: contexts/*/src, contexts/*/app/src, projects/*/src, packages/*/src - Infrastructure layer: Bundle
branch.action: Print: "layout.md missing — wrote default monorepo ## Layers (Source roots + Infrastructure layer=Bundle)."

substep.action: Bind LAYOUT_SOURCE_ROOTS (from the `- Source roots:` line) and LAYOUT_INFRA_LAYER (from the `- Infrastructure layer:` line) for the scaffold-phase `rules match` (substep 2.6b) and every downstream code-rule injection.
substep.rule: This is the ONLY layout.md write generation performs — it CREATES the file only when absent (a facet-a default), never modifies an existing discovery output. The match in 2.6b depends on these tokens, so 2.2b MUST precede it.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (SC-01..SC-13 + conditional SC-17..SC-24)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with the 13 base scaffold criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:420-434 (plus the conditional criteria below):

criteria.item: Root composer.json declares path repositories for every context/project/package (path-type repositories pointing at contexts/*, projects/*, packages/*), and a per-package composer.json exists for each package

Each monorepo component (contexts/*, the contexts/shared shared kernel, projects/*, packages/*) carries a schema-valid monorepo-component.json beside its composer.json (PHP-MONO-001 — the manifest the monorepo tooling wires)
composer install completes without errors (exit code 0)

Monorepo skeleton exists for every BC from discovery: contexts/{BC}/src/Domain/, contexts/{BC}/src/Application/, contexts/{BC}/src/Bundle/{Domain,Application}/ (Bundle is the infrastructure layer), plus contexts/{BC}/app/ for the framework layer, deployable projects/{app}/src/, and shared packages/

The deployable app is a real Symfony app rooted at projects/{app}: projects/{app}/src/Kernel.php exists declaring namespace App\<App> (e.g. App\Api), alongside projects/{app}/{config,public,bin}. The repo root has NO flat src/Kernel.php and NO root App\Kernel, and root composer.json autoload maps per-package (projects/*/src, contexts/*/src, packages/*/src), never a flat "App\\": "src/"

Shared-kernel context contexts/shared is LAYERED, not Share-only: contexts/shared/src/Domain AND contexts/shared/src/Application both exist alongside the Share kernel (contexts/shared/src/Share with DataType, Event, Exception, Messaging) — the published language every other context depends on
bin/console boots without errors (exit code 0) — the booted Kernel is the deployable projects/{app}/src/Kernel.php, not a flat root app (enforced by SC-03b + SC-09b)
vendor/bin/phpunit runs with zero tests, zero errors (exit code 0)

vendor/bin/phpstan analyse contexts/ projects/ packages/ --level=9 passes on empty project (exit code 0) — exercising the non-empty deployable projects/{app}/src (enforced by SC-09b), not an empty/flat root
vendor/bin/ecs check contexts/ projects/ packages/ passes on empty project (exit code 0)
vendor/bin/deptrac analyse passes with no violations on empty structure (exit code 0)

Deptrac's Project layer (the deployable collector over projects/*/src) is NON-EMPTY: at least one projects/*/src/*.php file exists, so a deptrac run on an empty-shell monorepo (vacuous Project layer) fails this criterion instead of trivially passing
.env.test exists with variables matching docs/architecture/test-environment.md
phpunit.xml has separate unit and integration test suites configured

Deptrac config has the per-context LAYER chain: Domain (depends on nothing + shared kernel), Application (depends on Domain + shared kernel), Bundle (infrastructure — depends on Domain + Application + shared kernel), app (framework — depends on Domain + Application + Bundle + shared kernel)

Deptrac config adds PACKAGE boundary edges: a context depends only on the shared-kernel context (contexts/shared) and packages/*, never on another context's internals; a deployable projects/{app} depends on the contexts it composes plus the shared kernel; packages/* are leaves (depend on nothing)
bin/ensure-test-stack.sh exists, is executable, runs: stack up → test-env migrations → test fixtures
Compose spec or .env.test pins APP_ENV=test for the E2E-facing service (E2E-ENV-CONV)
Playwright Chromium browser binary installed — npx playwright install --with-deps chromium exits 0
playwright.config.ts exists with trace retain-on-failure, screenshot only-on-failure, reporter [['list'],['html',{open:'never'}]], outputDir test-results, retries: 0, and test-results/ in .gitignore
playwright.config.ts defines a setup project (auth.setup.ts → playwright/.auth/*.json storageState) and an authenticated project with setup dependency (E2E-AUTH-STATE-CONV)
.env.test contains MAILER_DSN=null://null (Symfony recipe default, asserted by E2E-SIDEFX-CONV)
config/packages/test/messenger.yaml exists and routes transports to sync:// (E2E-SIDEFX-CONV)

packages/ui component-library stub exists: packages/ui/package.json, src/index.ts barrel entry, and one sample SFC src/components/BaseButton.vue — the shared-component home the vue pack's reuse-existing-shared-component rule (scoped packages/ui/**) points at

Twig templates tree exists: templates/base.html.twig base layout plus a templates/components/ partials subtree (e.g. templates/components/button.html.twig); NO packages/ui or playwright deliverable in twig mode

substep.rule: All 13 criteria plus SC-03b and SC-09b (plus SC-17/SC-18 when HAS_DATABASE, SC-19 when Playwright available, SC-20 when FRONTEND_MODE == vue, SC-21 when FRONTEND_MODE == vue AND N_auth_flows>=1, SC-23 when SIGNALS.has_mailer == yes, SC-24 when SIGNALS.has_messenger == yes, SC-25 when FRONTEND_MODE == vue, SC-26 when FRONTEND_MODE == twig) must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with 9 base CLI commands (incl. the SC-03b no-flat-Kernel + composer negated-grep and the SC-09b Project-layer-non-empty checks) (+ conditional SC-17/SC-18/SC-19/SC-20/SC-21/SC-23/SC-24 cmds) that verify the scaffold is functional:

commands.cmd: sh -c 'find contexts projects packages -maxdepth 2 -name monorepo-component.json | grep -q .'

substep.rule: These commands are emitted BARE (CMD-CONV): in local mode the daemon runs them on the host (Gate 0 having verified host Composer); in docker mode the daemon wraps them into the {{APP_SVC}} container started by task-R, so no host PHP/Composer is required.

SC-05 is the interpreter form `php bin/console`, NOT the bare entrypoint token `bin/console`. This still routes correctly — wrapcmd.classify() keys off the first token `php` (a phpToolPrefix) → classPHP, so RunTarget alone decides host-vs-container exactly as for any other PHP command; `php` does NOT pin it to the host. The interpreter form is deliberate: the skeleton is hand-scaffolded (not symfony/flex-generated), so `bin/console` is not guaranteed to carry a `#!/usr/bin/env php` shebang or the executable bit, and bare `bin/console` would then fail under `sh -c` with "Permission denied". SC-05 asserts the KERNEL boots, not that the entrypoint file is chmod +x — invoking via `php` proves the former without depending on the latter. Do NOT revert to bare `bin/console`.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config and fan-out hints
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Scaffold creates a bootable, TDD-ready Symfony DDD-MONOREPO skeleton: a root composer.json declaring path repositories for every package, per-package composer.json files, per-context contexts/{BC}/src/{Domain,Application,Bundle} + contexts/{BC}/app, the contexts/shared shared kernel, deployable projects/{app}, shared packages/, quality tools, and a deptrac.yaml enforcing both layer direction and package boundaries. All BC names come from docs/architecture/bounded-contexts.md. Composer packages from docs/architecture/libraries.md. Deptrac layer + package-edge definitions from the resolved php-symfony conventions (monorepo-layout.md / context-layers.md); project-specific overlay from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/bounded-contexts.md. Quality tool configs (PHPStan level 9, ECS, Deptrac) from .tmux-cli/templates/php-symfony/quality-gates.md. Test environment variables from docs/architecture/test-environment.md.

section.name: fan-out-hints
section.action: Build fan-out hints for multi-task execution (4 file-disjoint tasks; task-3 conditional on HAS_DATABASE; + task-R prepended when RUN_TARGET=docker per DOCKER-RUNTIME-FRONTLOAD):

task.name: Composer setup
task.scope: Root composer.json declaring path repositories for every context/project/package + a per-package composer.json for each package + a per-component monorepo-component.json beside each composer.json + composer install
task.owns: composer.json (root, path repositories), per-package composer.json (contexts/*/composer.json, projects/*/composer.json, packages/*/composer.json), per-component monorepo-component.json (contexts/*/monorepo-component.json, projects/*/monorepo-component.json, packages/*/monorepo-component.json), composer.lock, vendor/

task.name: Monorepo directory skeleton
task.scope: Create the monorepo skeleton: per-context contexts/{BC}/src/{Domain,Application,Bundle} + contexts/{BC}/app, the LAYERED contexts/shared shared kernel (contexts/shared/src/Domain + contexts/shared/src/Application alongside the contexts/shared/src/Share kernel), the deployable app rooted at projects/{app} with projects/{app}/src/Kernel.php (namespace App\<App>) plus projects/{app}/{config,public,bin} and NO flat root src/Kernel.php, and shared packages/, plus the conditional frontend skeleton — the packages/ui component-library stub (packages/ui/package.json + src/index.ts barrel + sample SFC, FRONTEND_MODE == vue) OR the Twig templates tree (templates/base.html.twig + templates/components/ partials, FRONTEND_MODE == twig), never both. After authoring the skeleton source the worker MUST run the quality gates over its OWN authored files against the now-present task-2 configs and leave them green: `vendor/bin/ecs check <own files> --fix` (self-conform — e.g. the ConcatSpaceFixer no-space-vs-spaced concat in projects/{app}/src/Kernel.php / projects/{app}/public/index.php), then `vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --level=9`, then `php bin/console` (boots — symfony/console is in the require set). As the LAST task in the 0→2→1 chain, task-1 owns end-to-end verification that SC-05/SC-07/SC-08/SC-09 are green over the assembled tree before reporting; it edits ONLY its own source, never task-2's configs
task.criteria: SC-03, SC-03b, SC-04, SC-05, SC-07, SC-08, SC-09, SC-25 (conditional FRONTEND_MODE == vue), SC-26 (conditional FRONTEND_MODE == twig)
task.owns: contexts/*/src/{Domain,Application,Bundle}/, contexts/*/app/, contexts/shared/src/, projects/*/src/, packages/*/, packages/ui/** (npm package.json+src, FRONTEND_MODE==vue), templates/** (FRONTEND_MODE==twig)
task.depends_on: task-0 (needs autoloader from composer install) AND task-2 (config-before-source: ecs.php / phpstan.neon / deptrac.yaml must exist so the skeleton is authored conformant and the cross-cutting gates can be verified before report)

task.name: Config files and quality tools
task.scope: phpunit.xml, phpstan.neon, ecs.php, deptrac.yaml, .env.test, APP_ENV=test pinning in Compose spec or .env.test (conditional on HAS_DATABASE, E2E-ENV-CONV SC-18), Playwright browser provisioning (conditional on Playwright available), playwright.config.ts + .gitignore test-results/ entry (conditional on FRONTEND_MODE == vue), playwright.config.ts setup project + authenticated project + playwright/.auth/ gitignore (conditional on FRONTEND_MODE == vue AND N_auth_flows>=1, E2E-AUTH-STATE-CONV SC-21), config/packages/test/messenger.yaml (conditional on SIGNALS.has_messenger == yes, E2E-SIDEFX-CONV SC-24)
task.criteria: SC-06, SC-07, SC-08, SC-09, SC-10, SC-11, SC-12, SC-13, SC-18 (conditional), SC-19 (conditional), SC-20 (conditional), SC-21 (conditional), SC-23 (conditional), SC-24 (conditional)

task.name: Ensure-stack script
task.scope: bin/ensure-test-stack.sh — three-phase stack-readiness script (stack up → test-env migrations → test fixtures)

section.rule: File-disjoint: task-0 owns every composer.json (root path-repositories + per-package) and every per-component monorepo-component.json beside it, task-1 owns the contexts/projects/packages directory skeleton plus the conditional frontend skeleton (packages/ui/** when FRONTEND_MODE == vue OR templates/** when FRONTEND_MODE == twig, never both — packages/ui is an npm package with NO composer.json, so it stays out of task-0's composer path-repo set), task-2 owns config root files (incl. deptrac.yaml with its layer + package edges), task-3 owns bin/ensure-test-stack.sh. task-R (docker mode) owns docker-compose.yaml + the dev Dockerfile. The task-1↔task-2 link is an ORDERING edge only (config-before-source) — task-1 edits solely its own source and runs `ecs --fix` only over its own files, never task-2's configs — so file-disjointness still holds. Zero overlap.

SC-05 (bin/console boots), SC-07 (PHPStan L9 over source), SC-08 (ECS over source) and SC-09 (Deptrac over the assembled tree) are CROSS-CUTTING — each needs the Composer packages (task-0), the source (task-1) AND the quality configs (task-2) together. They are therefore verified by task-1, which is ordered LAST (depends_on task-0 AND task-2) so every dependency exists at its runtime: task-1 self-conforms its own source with `ecs --fix` and confirms all four gates green before reporting. task-1 is intentionally NOT parallel with task-2 — config-before-source is deliberate ordering, not an oversight.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Composer check
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — composer.json is valid and all dependencies resolved

investigator.name: Kernel boot
investigator.fail: Kernel boot error or missing service configuration

investigator.name: Quality tools
investigator.commands: vendor/bin/phpstan analyse --level=9 && vendor/bin/ecs check contexts/ projects/ packages/ && vendor/bin/deptrac analyse
investigator.pass: All three commands exit 0 — zero errors, zero violations
investigator.fail: Any tool reports errors or violations

investigator.name: Test runner
investigator.pass: Exit 0, zero errors — test suites discovered and run successfully
investigator.fail: Test discovery failure, errors, or non-zero exit

section.rule: Minimum 4 investigators for scaffold (GM-08). Each has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.value: Application code, domain logic, controller actions, fixtures, E2E tests, Playwright setup — all handled by subsequent goals (goal-003+).

substep.title: Inject code rules (apply [CODE-RULE-INJECTION])
substep.action: Bind RULE_FILES = the comma-joined scaffold manifest+composer footprint that PHP-MONO-001 targets: the per-component manifests (contexts/*/monorepo-component.json, projects/*/monorepo-component.json, packages/*/monorepo-component.json) PLUS the per-package composer.json (contexts/*/composer.json, projects/*/composer.json, packages/*/composer.json). Bind RULE_PHASE = `scaffold` (this goal's phase token — pass it VERBATIM; do NOT remap it).

Run `tmux-cli rules match --files <RULE_FILES> --phase <RULE_PHASE> --json` and apply the spine's `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` procedure (<conventions> / <rule-to-goal-injection>): copy each NON-EMPTY `acceptance_line` into the ACCEPTANCE array (2.3); copy each `automated`/`mixed` `validate_cmd` into the VALIDATE array (2.4); fold any `review` `validate_cmd`s into ONE convention-audit investigator (the base is 4 from 2.5, so the 2–4 CAP applies — only ADD the investigator when a `must` review rule has a NON-EMPTY runnable `validate_cmd`, dropping the lowest keep-priority base investigator per the spine; otherwise inject NO investigator and let the review rule ride its acceptance_line + goal.md pointer); add the `Code rules: <ids> — read <paths>` line to CONTEXT (2.5). Empty `rules` ⇒ inject nothing.
substep.rule: `scaffold` IS a real Go rule phase: PHP-MONO-001 (phase=scaffold; globs **/monorepo-component.json + contexts|projects|packages/*/composer.json) matches this footprint, so its `CR-PHP-MONO-001` acceptance_line injects — GENUINE enforcement, NOT mechanical parity. Its review validate carries no runnable signal, so Go renders an empty `validate_cmd` ⇒ no validate row, no investigator, no drop. PRESERVE the 4 existing investigators.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Create bootable TDD-ready Symfony DDD-monorepo skeleton: path-repo composer, contexts/projects/packages, quality tools

ACCEPTANCE array from substep 2.3 (base 13 items SC-01..SC-13 plus SC-03b and SC-09b, +SC-17 when HAS_DATABASE, +SC-18 when HAS_DATABASE (G2), +SC-19 when Playwright available, +SC-20 when FRONTEND_MODE == vue, +SC-21 when FRONTEND_MODE == vue AND N_auth_flows>=1 (G6), +SC-23 when SIGNALS.has_mailer == yes (G7), +SC-24 when SIGNALS.has_messenger == yes (G7), +SC-25 when FRONTEND_MODE == vue, +SC-26 when FRONTEND_MODE == twig)

VALIDATE array from substep 2.4 (9 base commands — incl. the SC-03b no-flat-Kernel + composer negated-grep and the SC-09b Project-layer-non-empty checks — + conditional SC-17/SC-18/SC-19/SC-20/SC-21/SC-23/SC-24/25/26 cmds)
CONTEXT string from substep 2.5 (purpose + fan-out hints)
Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 2.5's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule in the spine <conventions> block. Scaffold lays down the repo-wide monorepo skeleton (root + per-package composer.json, contexts/**, projects/**, packages/**, config/, quality-tool configs) and runs before any BC goal, so OMIT scope (unknown ⇒ serialized — correct for the skeleton goal).

substep.rule: Description is 118 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 — scaffold has fewer independent failure categories than Gate 0.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Scaffold created: goal-002 (phase=scaffold, depends_on=[goal-001]) with 4 investigators, 4 fan-out tasks (task-3 conditional on HAS_DATABASE; + task-R when RUN_TARGET=docker), max_retries=3"
Proceed to Step 3 (when added by subsequent tasks).
